zh.lifeSummary: 當Christina de Corbet於1195年出生在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾馬可斯東時，她的父親Walter Corbet39歲，母親Alice De Valognes37歲。她和William de Dunbar至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1241年在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾馬可斯東馬可斯東 豪斯享年46歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Christina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Simplified form of Latin Christiana, feminine form of Christianus ( see Christian ), or a Latinized form of Middle English Christin ‘Christian’ (Old English christen, from Latin).

en.lifeSummary: When Christina de Corbet was born in 1195, in Makerstoun, Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Walter Corbet, was 39 and her mother, Alice De Valognes, was 37. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with William de Dunbar. She died in 1241, in Makerstoun House, Makerstoun, Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec William de Dunbar. ko.lifeSummary: Christina de Corbet(이)가 1195년 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르 마커스토우에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter Corbet(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice De Valognes(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 William de Dunbar(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1241년 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르 마커스토우 마커스토우 허스에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르에 묻혔다.
